MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-25-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Not contested, no appearances, and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Hartford Financial Services Group, on Behalf of Its Insured, Scherba's Auto Stores, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED.

Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that decedent was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Plaintiff Robert Brown testified that he accompanied his father to Scherba's Auto Store "30, maybe 40 times" and that his father purchased Fel Pro gaskets. (Hopwood Declaration, Ex. F (June 8, 2021 Robert Brown Deposition) at 37:3-8; 41:12-15.) He further testified that his father used products purchased from Scherba's on his own car or cars of family members, and removed gaskets purchased from Scherba's. (Id. at 40:21-43:3.) Mr. Brown also testified in his 2019 deposition that his father used a carbide scraper to remove head gaskets, intake gaskets, and exhaust gaskets in 1987 and 1988, and this work created particles and dust. (Van Aelstyn Decl., Ex. D (December 12, 2019 Robert Brown deposition) at 307:20-310:10.)

Plaintiff Brian Brown testified that he accompanied his father to Scherba's half a dozen times. (Hopwood Declaration, Ex. G (June 9, 2021 Brian Brown deposition) at 42:3-10.) He has a specific memory of purchasing gaskets for a '73 GMC Suburban and recalls being present when gaskets were removed for an engine change. (Id. at 43:21-45:11.) Plaintiff Dorenne Brown similarly testified that her husband made purchases at Scherba's Auto Store. (Van Aelstyn Decl., Ex. C (Dorenne Brown June 7, 2021 deposition) at 50:20-23.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-01-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jon C. James of HUGO PARKER, LLP (415-808-0300), appearing via CourtCall for Intervenor and moving party Landmark Insurance Company.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenor Landmark Insurance Company's Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855. Defendant failed to address Plaintiff Dorenne Brown's special interrogatory responses identifying Plaintiffs Dorenne Brown, Brian Brown, and Ronald Brown as persons with knowledge. (Declaration of Jon C. James, Exhibit D at 39:18-20). Defendant further failed to address Plaintiff's responses identifying co-worker witnesses Delores "Lolo" Galindez, Donald Canavan, Frederick Sherwood, Stephen Newman, Melvin Gladd, and Dave Huff. (Declaration of Jon C. James, Exhibit D at 36:15-17; 37:5-7.) As for co-worker Michael Sarao, Defendant failed to address his deposition testimony identifying Joe Ubeda, Fred Sherwood, and John De La Rosa as co-worker witnesses with knowledge. (Declaration of Jason Rose, Exhibit A at 83:20-86:24.) Sarao's inability to name the brand name, manufacturer, or supplier of the gaskets removed is insufficient to shift the burden. (Declaration of Jon C. James, Exhibit G at 60:12-61:5; Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal. App. 4th 1433, 1439.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for intervenors and moving party National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburg and TIG Insurance Company.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenors National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburg and TIG Insurance Company's Renewed Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Intervenors National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburg and TIG Insurance Company ("Intervenors") failed to sustain their initial burden pursuant to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiffs' responses to Intervenors' Special Interrogatories include specific facts about the late Ronald Brown's exposure to asbestos-containing products based on J&H Marine & Industrial Company's activities. Additionally, Intervenors' reliance on Andrews, to support their argument that Plaintiffs failed to identify any witnesses and facts known to those witnesses in their discovery responses is misplaced. (Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 148 Cal.App.4th 96, 102-107.) In contrast to Andrews, Intervenors' Special Interrogatory No. 6 did not request any information about facts that each witness possesses. (Id. at p. 105.) Instead, Special Interrogatory No. 6 asked Plaintiffs to identify persons "with knowledge of the facts." (James Decl., Ex. C, no. 6.) Intervenors fail to demonstrate that Plaintiffs do not have or cannot obtain evidence to support their claims. Intervenors did not depose any other witnesses to make an affirmative showing that Plaintiffs are unable to obtain the necessary evidence to support their claims. Accordingly, Intervenors' Motion for Summary Judgment is denied.

The Court declines to rule on the admissibility of Ronald Brown's deposition testimony as it was not material in determining that Intervenors are unable to meet their initial burden. The court did not consider the declaration of Michael Sarao, dated July 15, 2020. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion to Compel Person(s) Most Knowledgeable and Custodian(s) of Records for Defendant J&H Marine Industrial & Engineering Company, and to Compel Production of Documents is DENIED. Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing by CourtCall for plaintiff Ronald Brown.
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing by CourtCall for intervenor Landmark Incsurance Company.

RULING: PROPOSED INTERVENOR LANDMARK INSURANCE COMPANY, AS ALLEGED INSURER OF ROBERT E. BLAKE D/B/A GENERAL ENGINEERING & MACHINE, A DISSOLVED CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AN ANSWER IN INTERVENTION.

No argument is held. Ms. Chu requests that the Court allow a modification to its tentative ruling to allow plaintiff to add language that the denial as to plaintiff's request that they be reimbursed any duplicative discovery costs be denied "without prejudice" so that if plaintiff wishes that they may pursue those costs in a separate motion.

The Court granted Ms. Chu's request and adopted the tentative ruling as follows with the modification as requested: Proposed Intervenor Landmark Insurance Company's Motion for Leave to File an Answer in Intervention is GRANTED. Landmark must file its Answer in Intervention within 10 days of the entry of this order. Plaintiffs' request that Landmark be responsible, and reimburse Plaintiffs, for any duplicative discovery costs incurred by Plaintiffs as a result of Landmark's late appearance in this action is denied. Plaintiffs offer no authority for the proposition that intervenors are required to provide the requested disclosures. Plaintiff is to prepare a form of order incorporationg the modification. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: J. Alameda. Matter not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-20-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:

Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing by CourtCall for plaintiff Ronald Brown.
Heather Sweeney Kirkpatrick of HUGO PARKER, LLP (415-808-0300), appearing by CourtCall for defendant DCo, LLC, f/k/a Dana Companies. LLC.

The tentative ruling is contested. The matter is called and argument is held.

RULING: DEFENDANT DCO LLC, F/K/A DANA COMPANIES, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant DCo LLC f/k/a Dana Companies, LLC's Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant failed to address the documents, witnesses, and former testimony identified in Plaintiff's response to special interrogatory no. 47. (Defendant's Ex. E at 110-111.) Plaintiff's objections to Defendant's affirmative evidence, Exhibits F, G, H, I, and M, are sustained based on hearsay. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: J. Alameda. Court Reporter: Jennifer Roux, CSR #111033, Tel. 707.477.9599, Email: jenrouxcsr@gmail.com. (503/CML)
